name: Game Builder
concept: Events — your code waits, and runs the moment something happens

learn: You're not solving a puzzle now — you're BUILDING A GAME called Gem Rain 💎
Every 🎯 when block is a handler: code that waits, then runs the moment that thing happens
when ⬅️ is pressed, when you CATCH a gem, when you MISS — you decide what each one does
Nothing runs top-to-bottom here. The GAME calls your code. That's an event!
In real JavaScript these are functions the game calls back: function onCatch() { addScore(); }

blocks.name: when … blocks
blocks.text: the game runs whatever you tuck inside, the moment that event happens. Leave one empty and nothing happens for that event!

blocks.name: move hero
blocks.text: slide the hero one lane. Put these inside the arrow-key events!

blocks.name: score + 1 / lose a life
blocks.text: change the game's numbers when things happen

blocks.name: say / if / game over
blocks.text: show a message, check the game's state, or end the game

intro.goal: Wire up each event, press ▶ to test your game — then press 🎮 to actually PLAY the game you built!

recap: You built a real game — and you did it with events, the same way real apps work
An event handler is code that waits: the game calls onCatch the moment a gem lands on your hero
Events don't run in order — they run WHEN THEY HAPPEN. That's the big shift from World 1
You used everything: order inside handlers, ifs to check lives and score, variables the game keeps for you
Real JavaScript does exactly this: function onCatch() { addScore(); } — the game calls it back

quiz.q: What does a 🎯 when block do?
choices: waits, then runs when that thing happens
quiz.why: That's an event handler — it waits for its moment, then the game runs it.

quiz.q: You put nothing inside 🎯 when you MISS a gem. What happens on a miss?
quiz.why: An empty handler does nothing. Events only do what YOU put inside them.

quiz.q: 🎯 when you CATCH a gem → [🏆 score + 1]. You catch 4 gems. What's the score?
quiz.why: The handler runs once per catch — 4 catches, 4 points.

quiz.q: Who decides WHEN your onCatch code runs?
choices: the game, when a gem lands on you
quiz.why: That's the whole idea of events — the game calls your code back at the right moment.

quiz.q: 🧩 In World 4 you taught ⭐ moves. How is a 🎯 when block similar?
choices: both are functions — a bundle of blocks with a name
quiz.why: A handler IS a function! The difference: you call ⭐ yourself, the game calls onCatch for you.

quiz.q: 🎒 In World 5 the backpack remembered a number. What remembers your score here?
choices: the game keeps it — 🏆 score + 1 changes that variable
you have to count it yourself
quiz.why: Score is a variable the game keeps for you. score + 1 updates it, just like gems + 1 did.

quiz.q: 🤔 if 💔 no lives left? → [🛑 game over], inside when you MISS. When does the game end?
choices: on the miss that takes your last life
quiz.why: Every miss runs the handler, but the if only fires game over when lives hit 0.

quiz.q: Blocks inside ONE handler still run…
quiz.why: World 1 forever! Events decide WHEN a handler runs; inside it, order still rules.

levels.name: Take Control
levels.hint: NEW! Put ⬅️ move hero left INSIDE 🎯 when ⬅️ is pressed. Do the same for right. The test taps ⬅️ twice!

levels.name: Say Hello
levels.hint: 🎯 when the game starts → 💬 say GO! Every good game greets the player.

levels.name: Catch!
levels.hint: A gem is falling right on your hero! 🎯 when you CATCH a gem → 🏆 score + 1

levels.name: Ouch!
levels.hint: That gem falls far away — you'll miss it. Make a miss cost a life: 🎯 when you MISS → 💔 lose a life

levels.name: Chase the Rain
levels.hint: Three gems, three lanes! You need BOTH arrow events AND the catch event wired up for the test player to score.

levels.name: Cheer Them On
levels.hint: One handler can do TWO things: 🏆 score + 1 AND 💬 say NICE! Order still matters inside a handler.

levels.name: Three Lives
levels.hint: Two gems fall out of reach — each miss should cost a life AND say OOPS!

levels.name: Game Over
levels.hint: Every miss costs a life — but only the LAST one ends the game. Use 🤔 if 💔 no lives left? → 🛑 game over

levels.name: Winner!
levels.hint: Score, THEN check: 🤔 if 🏆 score high enough? → 💬 say YOU WIN! (3 gems wins this one)

levels.name: Boss: Your Whole Game
levels.hint: Boss! Wire up EVERYTHING: greet the player, move on both arrows, score + cheer on catches, lose a life on misses, and game over when lives run out 🏁
